# amux

> Open-source control plane for AI agents — run, monitor, and govern parallel agent sessions with a web dashboard, kanban board, notes, CRM, email, browser automation, skills, channels, scheduler, and agent-to-agent REST API orchestration. Self-healing, single-file, zero external dependencies. Currently supports Claude Code via tmux.

amux (Agent Multiplexer) is the open-source control plane for AI agent systems. It manages parallel agent sessions with self-healing, observability, cost control, and orchestration. Built-in tools include: markdown notes, CRM with interaction tracking, Mail.app email integration, shared Playwright browser automation, project-level slash-command skills, 1:1 inter-session channels with @mentions, a cron-style scheduler, and a file explorer with markdown editor. Currently supports Claude Code via tmux. MIT licensed. No cloud dependency. Single Python file, zero dependencies beyond python3 and tmux.

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/mixpeek/amux && cd amux && ./install.sh
amux register myproject --dir ~/Dev/myproject --yolo
amux start myproject
amux serve  # → https://localhost:8822
```

## Key Features

### Agent infrastructure
- Unlimited parallel Claude Code sessions via tmux (isolated panes, permanent conversation IDs)
- Self-healing watchdog: auto-compacts on context overflow, restarts on corruption, replays last message
- Channels: 1:1 inter-session messaging with @mentions for real-time agent coordination
- Kanban board with atomic task claiming via SQLite CAS (no Redis, no duplicated work)
- Agent-to-agent REST API: POST /api/sessions/NAME/send, GET /api/sessions/NAME/peek, POST /api/board/ID/claim
- Conversation fork: clone session JSONL history to a new agent on a new branch
- Token tracking from JSONL with deduplication across restarts

### Dashboard & mobile
- Web dashboard PWA — installable on iOS/Android, offline queue with Background Sync
- Native iOS app on the App Store
- File explorer with syntax highlighting and markdown editor with find-in-page

### Built-in tools
- Notes: full markdown documents with rich Quill editor, accessible via API across all sessions
- CRM: contacts with company, role, email, phone, LinkedIn, interaction logs, tags, and follow-up tracking
- Email integration: send and read email through the Mail.app API
- Browser automation: shared Playwright instance with saved auth profiles, screenshots, and AI agent mode
- Skills / slash commands: project-level custom commands (e.g. /commit, /review-pr) any agent can invoke
- Scheduler: named recurring jobs with cron expressions and management UI
- iCal feed for board items with due dates (S3 export for Google/Apple Calendar)

### Architecture
- Single-file architecture (amux-server.py) — no Docker, no build step, no config files
- MIT License — free, open-source, self-hosted

## How amux differs from plain Claude Code

Claude Code runs one agent per terminal. amux runs dozens — with live monitoring, mobile access, self-healing, and inter-agent coordination that don't exist in Claude Code itself. amux does not modify or wrap Claude Code; it manages tmux sessions that run it headlessly.

## Documentation

- [Documentation](https://amux.io/docs/): Complete reference for amux — installation, CLI commands (register, start, exec, stop, send, peek, attach, board), web dashboard, REST API for sessions/board/notes/CRM/email/browser/scheduler, kanban board with atomic task claiming, notes system, CRM with interaction logs, Mail.app email integration, Playwright browser automation with auth profiles, cron scheduler, skills/slash commands, inter-session channels, file explorer, self-healing watchdog, PWA mobile support, authentication, TLS/HTTPS, configuration (server.env, defaults.env, MCP), and architecture overview.

## Tools

- [AI Coding Agent Cost Calculator](https://amux.io/tools/ai-agent-cost-calculator/): Interactive calculator for estimating AI coding agent costs. Compare Claude Code Max plans ($100-200/mo) vs API keys (pay-per-token), adjust parallel agent count (1-50), hours per day, model mix (Opus vs Sonnet), and waste percentage. Shows monthly cost, daily cost, Max vs API breakeven, per-component cost breakdown, token efficiency visualization, and model routing savings (50-65%). Includes presets for solo developer, small team, agent fleet, and overnight batch workflows. Based on April 2026 Anthropic pricing.

## Guides

- [Anthropic Agentic Billing Change June 15, 2026: What Every Developer Must Know](https://amux.io/guides/anthropic-agentic-billing-june-2026/): Authoritative breakdown of Anthropic's June 15, 2026 billing restructuring. Verified facts: Claude Agent SDK, claude -p (non-interactive mode), Claude Code GitHub Actions, and third-party apps via Agent SDK move off subscription rate-limit bucket onto a separate monthly credit pool effective June 15. Credit amounts: Pro $20/month, Max 5x $100/month, Max 20x $200/month — all billed at standard API list prices. Interactive Claude Code terminal stays on subscription limits unchanged. After credits exhausted, usage bills as pay-as-you-go API charges. Includes: affected tools list (Agent SDK, claude -p, GitHub Actions, third-party SDK apps), cost math examples (daily scripts, overnight agent fleets, GitHub Actions at 50 PRs/month), Claude Sonnet 4.6 vs Opus 4.6 cost comparison (5× difference), seven-point pre-June-15 checklist, monitoring with amux token tracking, and FAQ.

- [Gemini CLI Is Ending June 18: Complete Migration Guide to Antigravity CLI](https://amux.io/guides/gemini-cli-to-antigravity-cli/): Authoritative migration guide for the June 18, 2026 Gemini CLI shutdown. Google is replacing Gemini CLI (Apache 2.0, `gemini` command, 1,000 req/day free) with Antigravity CLI (`agy` command, requires Google AI Pro subscription, closed-source). Verified facts: Antigravity available since May 19, 2026 (GA); Hooks, Agent Skills, and Subagents are fully retained; Extensions are renamed to Plugins; the 1M token context window is retained; the free tier ends. Step-by-step migration covering install, auth, shell script updates (`gemini` → `agy`), Extensions → Plugins conversion, custom TOML command migration, and amux session config updates. Feature comparison table: Gemini CLI vs Antigravity CLI vs Claude Code across command, model, context window, free tier, price, open source, hooks, subagents, extensions/plugins, SWE-bench, and IDE integration.

- [Best Claude Code Session Managers 2026 — 6 Tools Compared](https://amux.io/guides/best-claude-code-session-managers-2026/): The definitive 2026 comparison of every Claude Code session manager. Six tools rated across dashboard, self-healing, cost tracking, mobile access, inter-agent messaging, kanban board, REST API, and open-source availability: amux (web dashboard, self-healing watchdog, kanban with atomic task claiming, mobile PWA, REST API, MIT licensed, free/self-hosted); Claude Squad (terminal TUI, git worktrees, lightweight, MIT); Nimbalyst (native macOS app, GPU rendering, no external server); Opcode (web-based, hosted, no local install); Claude Code Agent Teams (experimental native feature, requires v2.1.32+, CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1, handles spawning but no dashboard/self-healing/cost tracking); DIY tmux scripts (zero dependencies, full control, breaks down at 5+ agents). Includes full feature matrix, billing analysis ($13/developer/active day baseline, parallel sessions multiply cost proportionally), decision framework by use case, and FAQ.

- [Agent Config Files Compared: CLAUDE.md vs .cursorrules vs AGENTS.md vs copilot-instructions](https://amux.io/guides/agent-config-files-compared/): Side-by-side comparison of every AI coding agent configuration file — CLAUDE.md (Claude Code), .cursorrules and .cursor/rules/ (Cursor), .github/copilot-instructions.md (GitHub Copilot), AGENTS.md (emerging vendor-neutral standard), .windsurfrules (Windsurf), .clinerules (Cline/Roo Code), and .aider.conf.yml (Aider). Feature matrix covering nested scoping, glob matching, hooks, permissions, skills, subagents, and MCP support. Three approaches for unified multi-tool repos: separate files, shared source, and script generation.

- [Claude Code Hooks Cookbook: 20 Production-Ready Recipes](https://amux.io/guides/claude-code-hooks-cookbook/): Copy-paste hook configs for Claude Code — block secret leaks, enforce formatting, auto-run tests, coordinate parallel agents, audit all tool calls, enforce conventional commits, and more. Each recipe includes the settings.json snippet and optional shell script. Covers PreToolUse (safety gates), PostToolUse (automation), Notification, and Stop events. Categories: security, code quality, automation, agent coordination, and workflow.

- [The Hybrid Stack: Running Claude Code + Cursor + Your IDE Together](https://amux.io/guides/claude-code-cursor-hybrid-workflow/): The average developer uses 2.3 AI coding tools. This guide covers the three-layer architecture (IDE + terminal agent + orchestrator) for running Claude Code and Cursor on the same codebase — task routing rules, conflict prevention with file routing/branches/worktrees, cost optimization across model tiers, daily workflow patterns, and the hybrid workflows for features, debugging, and major migrations.

- [AI-Generated Technical Debt: How to Use Agents to Clean Up After Agents](https://amux.io/guides/ai-generated-technical-debt/): 40% of AI-generated code gets rewritten within two weeks. Five types of AI debt (structural, dependency, test, documentation, integration), three-agent audit workflow, four-phase cleanup pipeline (quick wins, structural refactoring, test backfill, integration cleanup), scheduled nightly/weekly/monthly cleanup cycles, seven debt metrics with healthy targets, feedback loop for preventing new debt via CLAUDE.md rule generation, and ROI data showing 12-15% TCO reduction.
- [AI Agent Quality Gates: From Vibe Coding to Production-Ready Code](https://amux.io/guides/vibe-coding-quality-gates/): How to build a multi-agent validation pipeline for vibe-coded software. Four-agent architecture (implement, review, test, security), cross-model review pattern (60-70% more defects caught), safe AI code threshold data (25-40% sweet spot, 50%+ danger zone), seven effectiveness metrics, three pipeline configurations (3/5/8 agents), maturity model from raw vibe coding to self-improving pipeline, and anti-patterns to avoid.
- [A2A + MCP: How Agent-to-Agent and Model Context Protocol Work Together](https://amux.io/guides/a2a-mcp-agent-protocols/): How Google's A2A protocol and Anthropic's MCP work together in multi-agent systems. Protocol comparison table (transport, discovery, state model, auth, adoption), three integration patterns (MCP-wraps-A2A, A2A-for-orchestration, peer-to-peer), how amux maps to A2A concepts (session discovery, atomic task claiming, message passing, shared notes), architecture decision matrix (single agent through cross-org), and a six-step implementation checklist for multi-protocol agent systems.
- [AI Coding Agent Costs in 2026: The Complete Breakdown](https://amux.io/guides/ai-coding-agent-costs/): Real dollar figures for running AI coding agents. Claude Code Max ($100-200/mo) vs API keys (pay-per-token), per-hour cost tables for Opus vs Sonnet, model routing math (saves 50-65%), overnight fleet cost estimates ($50-600 for 10 agents), five hidden costs (context re-reading, compaction overhead, circular loops, redundant work, speculative tool calls), JSONL cost tracking setup, monthly budget planning by developer profile, and a 10-point optimization checklist ranked by impact.
- [How to Build a Multi-Agent Coding Orchestrator](https://amux.io/guides/multi-agent-orchestrator-architecture/): The architecture behind multi-agent coding systems — task boards with atomic claiming (SQLite CAS), inter-agent messaging, self-healing watchdogs, cost control, and the five-layer model (runtime, task distribution, communication, fault tolerance, governance). Compares Claude Code Agent Teams with full orchestration, covers real-world patterns (decomposition, triage, pipeline), and includes a 10-step implementation checklist.
- [How to Run a Parallel Security Audit with Claude Code Agents](https://amux.io/guides/parallel-security-audit/): Run 10 agents in parallel, each auditing a different module for security vulnerabilities. Covers decomposition by security boundary, focused threat-model prompts, triage workflow with a dedicated triage agent, comparison with traditional scanners (Semgrep, CodeQL, Snyk), recurring audit scheduling, and the fix-in-session workflow.
- [Claude Code Routines vs Self-Hosted Automation](https://amux.io/guides/claude-code-routines/): Claude Code Routines launched April 2026 with daily run caps and no persistent state. Compares Routines, OpenAI Codex background agents, and self-hosted automation with amux — with a decision matrix, cost comparison, and step-by-step migration walkthrough.
- [Claude Code Headless Mode — The Complete Self-Hosting Guide](https://amux.io/guides/claude-code-headless/): How to run Claude Code without the interactive terminal — for CI/CD, overnight batches, parallel agent fleets, and API-driven workflows. Covers --print and --output-format flags, Max subscription vs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY trade-offs, permission modes for unattended runs, cost tracking via JSONL transcripts, Docker patterns, and six common headless workflows.
- [What Is an Agent Control Plane?](https://amux.io/guides/agent-control-plane/): The missing infrastructure layer for AI agents. Five pillars — lifecycle management, observability, fault tolerance, orchestration, and governance — explained with the three-layer model (framework vs runtime vs control plane) and a comparison of the 2026 landscape.
- [How to Get 10x More Out of Your Claude Code Max Plan](https://amux.io/guides/claude-code-max-plan/): Rate limited by Wednesday? 7 strategies — model routing, parallel agents, context discipline, task pipelining, specs, API fallback, and scheduling — to 10x throughput within the same quota.
- [Agentic Engineering: The Practical Guide](https://amux.io/guides/agentic-engineering/): Go from 1 agent to 10+ running in parallel. Task decomposition, git isolation, coordination patterns, overnight operation, and review workflows. The step-by-step guide to the Karpathy-coined discipline.
- [CLAUDE.md Templates](https://amux.io/guides/claude-md-templates/): 20+ copy-paste CLAUDE.md configs for Python APIs, React apps, Go services, monorepos, data pipelines, Terraform, mobile apps, CLI tools. Filterable gallery with explanations.
- [Getting Started with amux](https://amux.io/guides/getting-started/): Install amux and run your first parallel Claude Code agents in under 5 minutes.
- [Running 10+ AI Coding Agents in Parallel (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/running-10-plus-agents/): How to run 10–50 parallel Claude Code, Codex, or Gemini CLI agents with amux. Covers machine requirements (RAM table: 4GB/5 agents → 64GB/50+ agents), three fleet organization patterns (by feature branch, by module, by task type), atomic kanban task claiming (POST /api/board/:id/claim prevents duplicated work), git conflict avoidance via worktrees, phone-based fleet monitoring via iOS app or PWA, self-healing overnight operation (auto-compact on context overflow, auto-restart on crash, last-message replay), and token spend management (per-session model routing, task-level budgets). HowTo JSON-LD (6 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (6 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [AI Agent Business Automations — Intent-Based, Not Brittle RPA (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/ai-agent-automations/): How amux runs business automations as AI-driven browser tasks — intent-resolved every run, not brittle coordinate replay. Covers the key difference from traditional RPA (intent vs pixel path), the three-tier execution ladder (API/CDP → browser vision agent → desktop computer-use), how automations compose with coding agents (scraper finds bugs → Claude Code session files PRs), what kinds of tasks work (financial reports, healthcare eligibility, competitor monitoring, status reconciliation), step-by-step setup (save auth profile → write SOP → run via browser agent API → schedule with amux scheduler), comparison table vs Zapier/Make/UiPath/n8n (8 rows), safety guardrails (stop on 2FA, confirm before irreversible, scoped auth, dry run), and the upcoming dedicated Automations tab. HowTo JSON-LD (5 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (6 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Self-Healing AI Agent Configuration (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/self-healing-configuration/): How amux keeps Claude Code agents running through crashes, context exhaustion, thinking-block corruption, and tool-approval blocks. Covers the four failure modes and their detection signals, YOLO mode (auto tool-approval via the unique 'Esc to cancel' marker), watchdog thresholds in ~/.amux/server.env (WATCHDOG_CONTEXT_THRESHOLD, WATCHDOG_IDLE_TIMEOUT), three recovery patterns (overnight run, monitor agent, git-safe restart), diagnostic table for common failure symptoms, launchd/systemd persistent service configs, and what self-healing does NOT handle (logic errors, rate limits, merge conflicts). HowTo JSON-LD (5 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (8 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Managed AI Agent Setup Service — Done-For-You AI Engineering (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/managed-ai-agent-setup/): What a managed AI agent setup service includes and when it makes sense vs building in-house. Covers the five onboarding phases (discovery, workflow design, agent configuration, system integration, ongoing management), system integration table (EHR/billing/CRM/accounting/email/browser by method and example), DIY vs managed decision matrix (6 scenarios), how managed AI differs from Zapier/Make/pre-built SaaS (flexibility, interaction model, scope ceiling), provider evaluation criteria (5 signals of a serious provider), amux Concierge overview, and the self-managed open-source path. Service JSON-LD + FAQPage JSON-LD (8 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Agent-to-Agent Orchestration](https://amux.io/guides/agent-to-agent-orchestration/): Make agents discover peers, delegate tasks, and coordinate via REST API.
- [Run AI Coding Agents from Your Phone — Mobile Management Guide (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/mobile-management-pwa/): amux is the only AI agent dashboard designed phone-first. Covers PWA installation on iPhone (Safari) and Android (Chrome), native iOS App Store app, remote access with Tailscale, the Steering queue (queue instructions to deliver at the agent's next turn boundary), self-healing overnight operation (no phone connection required), offline queue replay, and comparison table of desktop vs phone capabilities. HowTo + FAQPage structured data. Updated July 2026.
- [Token Spend Tracking](https://amux.io/guides/token-spend-tracking/): Monitor per-session AI token spend and daily costs.
- [Kanban Board for AI Agents — Atomic Task Coordination (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/kanban-board-for-agents/): How amux's SQLite-backed kanban board coordinates parallel AI coding agents without duplicated work. Covers the done vs verified status lifecycle (done=implemented, not confirmed in prod), atomic SQLite compare-and-swap claiming (POST /api/board/:id/claim — 200 success, 409 if already claimed), three multi-agent coordination patterns (shared queue / role-filtered pipeline / session-tagged assignments), CLAUDE.md integration template for board workflow, complete REST API reference (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/board, /claim, /clear-done, /api/calendar.ics), iCal integration for due dates with S3 export, CLI shorthands, and phone monitoring via iOS app and PWA. HowTo JSON-LD (5 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (6 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Conversation Forking](https://amux.io/guides/conversation-forking/): Clone a session's context into multiple new agents to explore different approaches.
- [Git Conflict Avoidance](https://amux.io/guides/git-conflict-avoidance/): Detect and prevent git conflicts when multiple agents work on the same repo.
- [REST API Reference](https://amux.io/guides/rest-api-reference/): Complete reference for amux's REST API — sessions, board, memory, sync, orchestration.
- [Scaling to 50+ AI Coding Agents — Architecture, Rate Limits & Monitoring (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/scaling-to-50-agents/): How to run 50+ Claude Code, Codex, or Gemini CLI agents in parallel. Covers hardware sizing (RAM table: 300-500MB/agent, 32GB for 50 agents), atomic task claiming via SQLite CAS, API rate limit strategies per provider, staggered starts, YOLO mode at scale, self-healing watchdog behavior at 50-agent scale (2-8 auto-recoveries per overnight run), tiled workspace monitoring, iOS app fleet monitoring, and a comparison table vs DIY scripts and cloud agent services. HowTo JSON-LD (5 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Multi-Runtime AI Agent Setup: Claude Code + Codex + Gemini CLI Together (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/multi-runtime-agent-setup/): How to configure and run all three major AI coding runtimes in one amux dashboard — Claude Code (Anthropic), Codex CLI (OpenAI), and Antigravity CLI (Google/Gemini). Provider comparison table (context window, cost, best task types), step-by-step installation and API key config, session registration per provider, task routing matrix by provider strength (Claude for architecture/review, Codex for generation/tests, Gemini for large-context analysis), rate limit distribution across provider buckets (2-3× throughput vs single-provider), and cost optimization strategy (Gemini Flash for docs/tests at 30× cheaper than Claude Sonnet). HowTo JSON-LD (5 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (4 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Remote Access via Tailscale](https://amux.io/guides/remote-access-tailscale/): Securely access your agent fleet from anywhere.
- [Session Memory and Context](https://amux.io/guides/session-memory-and-context/): Per-session and global memory files for persistent agent context.
- [Setting Up YOLO Mode](https://amux.io/guides/setting-up-yolo-mode/): Configure auto-approval for unattended headless runs.
- [Using MCP Servers](https://amux.io/guides/using-mcp-servers/): Connect Claude Code to external tools via MCP in amux sessions.
- [Workspace Tiled Layout](https://amux.io/guides/workspace-tiled-layout/): Multi-pane tiled view for monitoring several sessions simultaneously.
- [Cost Optimization](https://amux.io/guides/cost-optimization/): Reduce token spend across parallel agent sessions.
- [How to Run an AI Engineering Team (2026 Guide)](https://amux.io/guides/team-workflow-setup/): How to run an AI engineering team with amux — from solo developer with a fleet of agents to multi-developer shared server. Covers naming conventions, board coordination, remote access, mobile management, and the full team setup workflow. Includes comparison of per-developer vs shared-server deployment models.
- [Run Claude Code in the Background: amux start --detach (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/detached-agent-sessions/): How to start Claude Code and other AI coding agents in detached (background) mode. amux start --detach / -d creates the tmux session without attaching, returning immediately. Enables boot scripts (launchd/systemd), justfiles, SSH one-shots, CI pipelines, and overnight batch runs. Covers the flag semantics, monitoring from phone, self-healing interaction, and code examples for macOS launchd plist, justfiles, and SSH remote triggers. HowTo JSON-LD (4 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Added July 2026.
- [Expose a Localhost Port to the Public Internet with amux Tunnel (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/expose-localhost-publicly/): How to use amux tunnel to get a stable public HTTPS URL for any local port. Machine dials out to the amux cloud relay — no inbound firewall port, no router config. URL format: https://<id>.t.amux.io/ where id = sha256(token)[:16], stable across restarts. Use cases: webhook testing, sharing dev servers with clients/teammates, OAuth redirect URIs, mobile device testing, exposing the amux dashboard remotely. Comparison table vs ngrok (SSE=No, WebSocket=No, TCP=No, inspection UI=No, custom domains=No). Honest about limitations and the public (unguessable but unauthenticated) nature of the URL. Requires amux cloud (paid). HowTo JSON-LD (5 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Added July 2026.
- [AI Agent Fleet Operations — Bulk Actions, Real-Time Monitoring & Fleet-Level Visibility (2026)](https://amux.io/guides/agent-fleet-operations/): How to operate a fleet of 10-50 AI coding agents with amux — bulk continue all idle sessions, inspect individual subagent transcripts, load historical output, and monitor from your phone. Covers: fleet health view (status dot semantics: green/yellow/red), bulk-actions modal with "Continue all idle sessions" (v0.9.78 — wakes up entire stalled fleet in one tap), per-agent peek panel, subagent transcript switcher ▲/▼ navigation (v0.9.79/v0.9.83), Load earlier output scrollback (v0.9.82), responsive peek that reflows to phone width (v0.9.85), and 8-row comparison table (fleet ops with amux vs. without). Targets: "continue all idle Claude Code sessions", "bulk restart AI coding agents", "Claude Code fleet management". HowTo JSON-LD (5 steps) + FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Added July 2026.

## Features

- [Self-Healing AI Agents — amux Watchdog & Auto-Recovery (2026)](https://amux.io/features/self-healing/): How amux keeps Claude Code agents alive overnight. Four failure modes covered: context exhaustion (auto /compact), thinking-block corruption (detect + restart + replay), tool-approval stuck (YOLO mode auto-approve), silent hang (idle timeout kick). Watchdog thresholds in ~/.amux/server.env. Last-message replay ensures agents pick up where they left off after a crash. Comparison table: 5 failure scenarios, with vs without amux self-healing. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs) + Article JSON-LD + BreadcrumbList. Updated July 2026.
- [AI Agent Dashboard — amux Web Dashboard & iOS App (2026)](https://amux.io/features/web-dashboard/): The amux dashboard covers sessions (live status dots, token spend, plan strip), peek panel (SSE-streamed terminal output, subagent switcher ▲/▼, load earlier output scrollback, steering bar), board (SQLite kanban with atomic CAS claiming), notes, CRM, scheduler, and calendar. Mobile: native iOS app (id6760410435) + PWA. SSE backbone — all clients see changes within ~2s. Comparison table vs DIY tmux / Claude Code alone / Devin (7 capabilities). FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [AI Agent Coordination — amux REST API & Atomic Task Claiming (2026)](https://amux.io/features/agent-coordination/): Every amux session gets $AMUX_URL and $AMUX_SESSION at startup. Coordination primitives: atomic SQLite CAS board claim (POST /api/board/:id/claim — 200 if won, 409 if taken), inter-session messaging (POST /api/sessions/:name/send), peer output reading (GET /api/sessions/:name/peek), global memory, and agent discovery (GET /api/sessions). Common patterns: producer-consumer, pipeline, role-based routing. Comparison table vs LangGraph/file-based/Redis (5 approaches). FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Mobile PWA & iOS App — Manage AI Agents from Your Phone (2026)](https://amux.io/features/mobile-pwa/): Native iOS app (App Store id6760410435) + installable PWA for Android. Full dashboard on 375px screen: fleet scan, peek panel, plan strip, subagent switcher, load earlier output, steering bar, board, notes, CRM, scheduler. Touch targets ≥44×44px, safe-area-inset-* for iOS notch, responsive peek reflows to phone width. Offline queue via Background Sync. Remote access: Tailscale (recommended) or amux tunnel (cloud). Comparison table: iOS app vs PWA vs SSH+tmux vs Devin mobile. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [Single-File Architecture — amux is One Python File (2026)](https://amux.io/features/single-file-architecture/): amux-server.py (~35k lines) contains HTTP server (stdlib only), REST API (~60 endpoints), SSE backbone, full dashboard HTML/CSS/JS (inline), SQLite schema, self-healing watchdog, email/CRM/browser/iCal/tunnel. Zero external Python deps — requires only Python 3 and tmux. Self-restarts on file save via os.execv. Contributing: one PR, one file. Deployment comparison table vs typical web app / Docker / cloud agents. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Updated July 2026.
- [amux Tunnel — Expose localhost to the internet](https://amux.io/features/tunnel/): amux tunnel is a built-in HTTP/HTTPS relay that gives your local amux dashboard a stable public URL at https://<id>.t.amux.io/. Machine dials out to the amux cloud gateway — no port forwarding, no firewall changes, no router configuration needed. URL is stable across restarts (derived from token via sha256[:16]). Use cases: mobile dashboard access, webhook testing, OAuth redirect URIs, team demo sharing, CI/CD integration. Limitations: HTTP only, no WebSocket/SSE/TCP, no custom domains, no free tier (requires amux Cloud $20/mo). 7-row comparison table vs ngrok/Cloudflare Tunnel/localtunnel/SSH reverse tunnel/self-host gateway. Self-hosting instructions in cloud/gateway/ (Terraform + GCP). Security: add dashboard password to protect the public URL. BreadcrumbList + Article + FAQPage JSON-LD (7 Qs). Added July 2026.
- [amux Cloud — Tunnel relay for local AI agents](https://amux.io/cloud/): amux Cloud adds exactly one feature beyond the free open-source version: the tunnel relay that gives your local dashboard a stable public HTTPS URL. Everything else (dashboard, agent orchestration, kanban, CRM, email, browser automation, scheduler, mobile PWA, self-healing, MCP) is free MIT-licensed open source. Pricing: $20/month or $200/year. Full OSS vs Cloud feature matrix (14 rows). Self-hosting: tunnel gateway open source in cloud/gateway/ with Terraform + setup script (typically $5-10/mo VM cost). amux Cloud is NOT a cloud AI execution environment — agents run locally, no data on amux servers. FAQ comparing to Devin/Codex/Jules (cloud AI ≠ amux Cloud). BreadcrumbList + WebPage + FAQPage JSON-LD (6 Qs). Added July 2026.

## Use Cases

- [AI Coding While You Sleep](https://amux.io/use-cases/ai-coding-while-you-sleep/): Queue work before bed, wake up to completed pull requests.
- [Parallel Feature Development](https://amux.io/use-cases/parallel-feature-development/): Run independent features in separate agents simultaneously.
- [Automated Code Review](https://amux.io/use-cases/automated-code-review/): Dedicated agents that review PRs and suggest improvements.
- [Large-Scale Refactoring](https://amux.io/use-cases/large-scale-refactoring/): Parallelize refactoring across modules with conflict avoidance.
- [Test Generation at Scale](https://amux.io/use-cases/test-generation-at-scale/): Multiple agents generating tests for different modules simultaneously.
- [Bug Triage and Fixing](https://amux.io/use-cases/bug-triage-and-fixing/): Agents claim bugs from the kanban board and work in parallel.
- [Documentation Generation](https://amux.io/use-cases/documentation-generation/): Dedicated agents keeping docs in sync with code changes.
- [Monorepo Management](https://amux.io/use-cases/monorepo-management/): One agent per package in a monorepo, coordinated via the board.
- [Dependency Updates](https://amux.io/use-cases/dependency-updates/): Agents that update, test, and PR dependency bumps across a codebase.
- [API Endpoint Generation](https://amux.io/use-cases/api-endpoint-generation/): Parallelize endpoint implementation across multiple agents.
- [Security Audit](https://amux.io/use-cases/security-audit/): Agents scanning different attack surfaces simultaneously.
- [Code Migration](https://amux.io/use-cases/code-migration/): Migrate language or framework across a codebase in parallel chunks.
- [Research and Prototyping](https://amux.io/use-cases/research-and-prototyping/): Run parallel prototype branches, compare results.
- [CI/CD Pipeline Creation](https://amux.io/use-cases/ci-cd-pipeline-creation/): Agents that build and validate CI/CD pipelines.
- [Legacy Code Modernization](https://amux.io/use-cases/legacy-code-modernization/): Modernize legacy code across files in parallel.

## For — Roles & Personas

- [amux for Solopreneurs](https://amux.io/for/solopreneurs/): One person, one dashboard, a fleet of coding agents. amux lets solopreneurs run what amounts to a 10-person engineering team — with CRM, email, browser automation, and scheduler built in. Covers agent role breakdown (developer, reviewer, test, docs, bug triage), business operations beyond coding (CRM, Gmail integration, browser automation), managing the fleet from your phone, and the /concierge/ managed setup option.

- [amux for AI Engineers — Workflow Orchestration, Multi-Model Routing &amp; Harness Engineering (2026)](https://amux.io/for/ai-engineers/): AI engineers use amux as their agent control plane — deploying it once and building their entire agent infrastructure on top. Covers how amux differs from SDK-based orchestrators (OS process isolation vs shared event loop), what AI engineers build on top (multi-agent pipelines, multi-model routers, observability dashboards, self-healing pipelines, MCP fleet integration, agent-to-agent orchestration, CI/CD integration), harness engineering patterns (context budgets, compaction triggers, memory files), multi-model routing table (Opus for architecture/review, Sonnet for standard coding, Haiku for test/docs), JSONL transcript parsing for custom observability, MCP server fleet config via centralized mcp.json, self-healing pipeline design (idempotent tasks, board as circuit breaker, monitor agent pattern), agent-to-agent REST API (send/peek/fork), CI/CD GitHub Actions integration example, security model (YOLO mode, hooks, allowed paths, TLS), comparison table vs LangGraph/CrewAI/Claude Agent Teams/Devin (8 rows). FAQPage JSON-LD (6 Qs). Updated July 2026.

- [amux for Startup Founders — Run an AI Engineering Team Without Hiring (2026)](https://amux.io/for/startup-founders/): Startup founders use amux to coordinate a fleet of Claude Code, Codex, or Gemini CLI agents that build features, fix bugs, write tests, and draft docs in parallel — managed from one dashboard or their phone, with no headcount. Covers what a parallel agent fleet looks like in practice (5-session example table), from idea to shipped feature in one day workflow, built-in business tools (CRM, Gmail, browser automation, notes, scheduler), amux vs. hiring vs. managed-service cost comparison table, open-source self-hosted pricing model ($0 to amux, pay only API tokens), FAQ for non-technical founders, and Concierge managed onboarding option. Updated July 2026.

- [amux for Indie Hackers — Run an AI Engineering Team Solo (2026)](https://amux.io/for/indie-hackers/): Indie hackers use amux to run a 5-10 agent engineering team from their phone — shipping features overnight, automating customer support with built-in Gmail API, tracking contacts in the CRM, and building products that compound while they sleep. Covers the typical indie hacker week (Sunday night setup → Monday morning review), three team patterns (Weekend Shipper, Daily Autopilot, Sprint Machine), cost math for solo builders ($15-40/night API vs $200/month Max plan vs $400-800/night freelancer), beyond-coding tasks table (support → Gmail, market research → browser automation, CRM, scheduler, content), overnight agent workflow checklist (atomic tasks, separate branches, YOLO mode, CLAUDE.md), and getting-started path. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). Updated July 2026.

## Comparisons

- [amux vs Hermes Agent](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-hermes-agent/): NousResearch's open-source self-improving personal AI daemon (175k GitHub stars, Feb 2026) vs amux parallel coding fleet. Hermes = single persistent agent with cross-session memory, skill self-improvement, 16+ messaging platform connectors, any LLM including local. amux = 10-50 parallel coding agents (Claude Code/Codex/Gemini CLI), shared kanban with atomic claiming, self-healing overnight operation, mobile fleet management. Different jobs: depth for one agent vs throughput for many. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). July 2026.
- [amux vs Cowork](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-cowork/): Claude Cowork (Anthropic's single computer-use agent, available on paid Claude plans) vs amux (parallel coding fleet, open source). Cowork = one agent, GUI/computer-use tasks, desktop app, Claude only. amux = unlimited parallel sessions, coding-agent specific (Claude Code/Codex/Gemini CLI), self-healing watchdog, mobile management, free + API tokens. Throughput comparison: 20 tasks in 1hr (10 parallel amux sessions) vs 10hr (Cowork sequential). FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). July 2026.
- [amux vs OpenClaw](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-openclaw/): OpenClaw (formerly Warelay/Moltbot, 347k GitHub stars — most starred repo in history, MIT, OpenAI-backed) vs amux. OpenClaw = messaging-native personal assistant (WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Discord/iMessage/20+ more), 100+ AgentSkills, low technical floor, single agent. amux = developer fleet dashboard (web PWA + iOS app), parallel coding agents, kanban coordination, git-safe worktrees, overnight self-healing. Complementary tools for different audiences. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). July 2026.
- [amux vs Claude Managed Agents](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-claude-managed-agents/): Self-hosted alternative to Anthropic's April 2026 Managed Agents launch. $0 session fees vs $0.08/session-hour, model-agnostic, MIT-licensed.
- [amux vs Claude Code Agent Teams](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-claude-code-agent-teams/)
- [amux vs Cursor](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-cursor/)
- [amux vs Aider](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-aider/): Aider (open-source AI pair programmer, ~25k stars, Apache 2.0, multi-LLM) vs amux. Aider = single-session interactive pair programming with excellent diff quality, 20+ LLM providers. amux = parallel fleet for unattended overnight runs, Claude Code-native, web dashboard, self-healing watchdog. Complementary: Aider for surgical interactive edits, amux for batch fleet runs. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). July 2026.
- [amux vs GitHub Copilot](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-github-copilot/): GitHub Copilot (~1.8M paid users, $10-19/mo, GPT-4o/4.5, IDE-integrated) vs amux. Copilot = inline completions + chat inside VS Code/JetBrains/Vim, synchronous interactive loop. amux = autonomous parallel fleet (10-50 sessions overnight), unattended, self-healing, mobile dashboard. Different layers of the stack — most developers use both. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). July 2026.
- [amux vs Jules (Google)](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-jules/): Google's Jules autonomous coding agent (cloud-hosted, Gemini models, GitHub Issues → PR, one task at a time) vs amux (self-hosted, model-agnostic, 10-50 parallel agents, open source). Jules = managed cloud micro-service for issue-to-PR automation. amux = open-source control plane for fleet-scale parallel operation. Feature matrix: parallel agents (10-50 vs 1), model choice (any vs Gemini only), self-hosted (yes vs no), open source (MIT+CC vs no), built-in CRM/email/browser (yes vs no). When to use each + cost comparison table. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). July 2026.
- [amux vs OpenAI Symphony](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-openai-symphony/): OpenAI Symphony (open-sourced June 2026, ticket-driven Codex orchestrator, Elixir/OTP, requires Linear) vs amux (Claude Code fleet, Python, built-in SQLite kanban, web dashboard). Symphony = fully autonomous pipeline, no dashboard, Linear-only. amux = developer cockpit with observability, mobile management, 5-min setup. FAQPage JSON-LD (5 Qs). July 2026.
- [amux vs Devin](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-devin/)
- [amux vs GitHub Copilot](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-github-copilot/)
- [amux vs Windsurf](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-windsurf/)
- [amux vs OpenHands](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-openhands/)
- [amux vs DIY tmux Scripts](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-diy-tmux/)
- [amux vs CrewAI](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-crewai/)
- [amux vs Cline (Roo Code)](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-cline/)
- [amux vs Goose](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-goose/)
- [amux vs OpenAI Codex](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-codex/)
- [amux vs sandboxed.sh](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-sandboxed-sh/)
- [amux vs Claude Code Web UIs](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-claude-code-web-ui/)
- [amux vs LangGraph](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-langraph/)
- [amux vs Bolt.new](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-bolt-new/)
- [amux vs Replit Agent](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-replit-agent/)
- [amux vs v0 (Vercel)](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-v0/)
- [amux vs AutoGen](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-autogen/)
- [amux vs Claude Desktop](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-claude-desktop/)
- [amux vs Antigravity CLI (agy)](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-antigravity-cli/): Antigravity CLI replaced Gemini CLI on June 18, 2026. amux runs and manages multiple agy sessions in parallel — self-healing, kanban, and mobile monitoring built in.

## For Specific Audiences

- [amux for Freelancers](https://amux.io/for/freelancers/): How freelance developers use amux to run one coding agent per client project in parallel — billing more hours while working fewer, managing clients with the built-in CRM, and delivering overnight. Updated July 2026.
- [amux for Engineering Managers](https://amux.io/for/engineering-managers/): How engineering managers deploy amux to multiply team velocity without adding headcount — shared dashboard across all developer agent sessions, built-in kanban board, overnight batch delivery, phone monitoring. Updated July 2026.
- [amux vs Plandex](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-plandex/): Comparison of amux (multi-agent fleet control plane) and Plandex (single-task long-running coding engine). Parallel fleet vs sequential execution, mobile-first vs CLI-only. Updated July 2026.
- [amux vs Zed](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-zed/): Comparison of amux (multi-agent fleet orchestrator) and Zed (collaborative AI code editor). Different tools for different jobs — use both or choose based on your workflow. Updated July 2026.
- [amux vs ngrok — ngrok alternative with stable URL](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-ngrok/): Honest comparison of amux tunnel and ngrok for exposing localhost publicly. ngrok wins on custom domains, TCP, and inspection UI. amux wins on stable URL without a paid ngrok plan and zero separate tool to install. Updated July 2026.
- [Monitor Your Claude Code Agents (Plan Strip Guide)](https://amux.io/guides/claude-code-plan-monitoring/): How to use amux's Plan strip, peek panel, and dashboard board to see exactly what your Claude Code agents are doing in real time. Covers v0.9.44 Plan strip, saved messages, quick-action chips. Updated July 2026.
- [AI Coding While You Sleep](https://amux.io/use-cases/ai-coding-while-you-sleep/): Complete guide to running 10-50 Claude Code agents overnight with amux — self-healing watchdog, YOLO mode, morning board review from your phone. Full FAQ and how-to. Updated July 2026.
- [Conversation Forking Guide](https://amux.io/guides/conversation-forking/): How to fork a Claude Code session's full context into multiple parallel branches — explore three implementations at once, A/B test architectures, or branch at a risky decision point. Updated July 2026.
- [AI Agents and Google Calendar Integration](https://amux.io/use-cases/ai-agent-google-calendar/): How amux syncs your AI agent schedules to Google Calendar — see what your AI engineering team is working on today, right next to your human team's calendar. Updated July 2026.

## Press

- [Press Kit](https://amux.io/press/): Official press kit — product description, boilerplate copy, key stats (MIT+CC, ~30k LOC single Python file, iOS App Store, 2025 launch), screenshots, and media contact (ethan@mixpeek.com). FAQ for journalists: what problem amux solves, who built it, pricing, and how it differs from Cursor and Devin.

## Curated Lists

- [Curated Lists Hub](https://amux.io/lists/): Index of ranked, research-backed lists of AI tools, coding agents, orchestrators, and developer frameworks.
- [Best AI Coding Agents 2026](https://amux.io/lists/best-ai-coding-agents-2026/): 10 tools ranked — Claude Code (#1), amux (#2 as fleet orchestrator), Codex CLI, Cursor, Devin, Cline, Aider, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, Gemini CLI. Pricing, strengths, summary table.
- [Best Tools for Claude Code Users 2026](https://amux.io/lists/best-claude-code-tools-2026/): amux fleet manager, claude-squad, essential MCP servers, CLAUDE.md patterns, slash command skills, git worktrees, iTerm2 integration, standing instructions.
- [Best AI Agent Orchestrators 2026](https://amux.io/lists/best-ai-agent-orchestrators-2026/): amux, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI Symphony, Claude Managed Agents, Hermes Agent, dmux, OpenHands, sandboxed.sh — architecture, OSS status, when to use.
- [Best Open Source AI Coding Tools 2026](https://amux.io/lists/open-source-ai-coding-tools-2026/): OSS-only — amux, Aider, Cline/Roo Code, OpenHands, Continue.dev, Goose, Antigravity CLI. License, language, model provider.
- [Best AI Tools for Solopreneurs 2026](https://amux.io/lists/ai-tools-for-solopreneurs-2026/): 10 tools — what each replaces, monthly cost, time saved. amux, Claude, Cursor, Notion AI, Copilot, Perplexity, Make, v0, Bolt.new, Linear AI.
- [AI Agent Frameworks Compared 2026](https://amux.io/lists/ai-agent-frameworks-comparison-2026/): amux, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, Bee Agent, Agency Swarm, MetaGPT — language, multi-agent, state management, best use cases.
